a.xlink:title: Base exception class for BC errors.
Base exception for capping surface (cap VTP) errors.
Exception thrown when a requested array is not found.
Exception thrown when VTP file cannot be read or is invalid.
Exception thrown when a requested flag is not defined.
Exception thrown when a global node ID is not found in the global-to-local map.
Exception thrown when node count mismatch between VTP and face.
Exception thrown when a node ID is out of range.
Exception thrown when BoundaryCondition is not properly initialized.
Exception thrown when face_ is nullptr during distribute.
Exception thrown when a point cannot be found in VTP file.
Exception thrown when array validation fails.
Exception thrown when a VTP array has incorrect dimensions.
Exception thrown when a VTP file doesn't contain a required array.
Cap mesh shares no nodes with the coupled boundary face.
Cap VTP file cannot be opened.
Cap VTP connectivity does not match expected TRI3 topology.
Cap face quadrature (shape functions on TRI3) setup failed.
Cap VTP uses an unsupported cell type (only TRI3 is supported).
VTP read, parse, or validation error during cap load.
Base exception type for errors originating in the FE library.
A required dependency is missing or failed to load.
An index is outside its valid range.
A requested operation or feature is not implemented.
A parsing or input-format error.
Global assembly failure.
Failure reported by a linear-algebra or solver backend.
Iterative process failed to converge.
Degree-of-freedom numbering or lookup failure.
An argument failed validation.
Unsupported or malformed element request.
Required initialization step has not been performed.
Element geometric mapping is singular or inverted.
Base exception type for errors originating in the Basis module.
Invalid Basis request or configuration.
Internal basis construction or transform setup failure.
Requested element topology is incompatible with the basis family.
Basis evaluation request cannot be satisfied.
Public-to-canonical node ordering or coordinate lookup failure.
